prerequisites: An Xsolla Publisher Account with access to the project you want to pull data from.
A project created in Publisher Account whose Project ID you can read (provided to the connector as project_id).
Permission in the account to open Company settings or Project settings and create an API key.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Xsolla server-to-server API calls authenticate with HTTP Basic auth: the Authorization header carries a Base64-encoded project_id:api_key pair. The connector takes the secret api_key and combines it with project_id to form this credential. The API key is confidential and must be stored on your server, never in client applications or on the frontend.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Project ID
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Publisher Account and open the project you want to sync, then go to Project settings > General settings. Your project_id is shown there. Record it to supply as the connector's project_id field.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: In Publisher Account, go to Company settings > API keys or Project settings > API keys, then create a new API key. Copy the key value when it is shown, since it is displayed only once and is treated as confidential.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key as api_key and your project identifier as project_id. The connector uses these to build the Base64-encoded project_id:api_key Basic auth credential sent in the Authorization header on each request.

sources.title: Xsolla API Documentation - General questions (API key, project ID, merchant ID)
